passages.title: Katherine Mansfield
passages.source_html:
Katherine Mansfield


Katherine Mansfield was a modernist writer of short fiction who was born and brought up in New Zealand


A


Katherine Mansfield Beauchamp Murry was born in 1888, into a prominent family in Wellington, New Zealand. She became one of New Zealand's best-known writers, using the pen name of Katherine Mansfield.  The daughter of a banker, and born into a middle-class family, she was also a first cousin of Countess Elizabeth von Arnim, a distinguished novelist in her time. Mansfield had two older sisters and a younger brother. Her father, Harold Beauchamp, went on to become the chairman of the Bank of New Zealand. In 1893, the Mansfield family moved to Karori, a suburb of Wellington, where Mansfield would spend the happiest years of her childhood; she later used her memories of this time as an inspiration for her Prelude story.


B


Her first published stories appeared in the High School Reporter and the Wellington Girls' High School magazine in 1898 and 1899.  In 1902, she developed strong feelings for a musician who played the cello, Arnold Trowell, although her feelings were not, for the most past, returned. Mansfield herself was an accomplished cellist, having received lessons from Trowell's father. Mansfield wrote in her journals of feeling isolated to some extent in New Zealand, and, in general terms of her interest in the Maori people (New Zealand's native people), who were often portrayed in a sympathetic light in her later stories, such as How Pearl Button Was Kidnapped.


C


She moved to London in 1903, where she attended Queen's college, along with her two sisters. Mansfield recommenced playing the cello, an occupation that she believed, during her time at Queen's, she would take up professionally.  She also began contributing to the college newspaper, with such a dedication to it that she eventually became its editor. She was particularly interested in the works of the French writers of this period and on the 19th-century British writer, Oscar Wilde, and she was appreciated amongst fellow students at Queen's for her lively and charismatic approach to life and work . She met fellow writer Ida Baker, a South African, at the college, and the pair became lifelong friends. Mansfield did not actively support the suffragette movement in the UK. Women in New Zealand had gained the right to vote in 1893.


D

Mansfield first began journeying into the other parts of Europe in the period 1903-1906, mainly to Belgium and Germany. After finishing her schooling in England, she returned to her New Zealand home in 1906 , only then beginning to write short stories in a serious way. She had several works published in Australia in a magazine called Native Comparison, which was her first paid writing work , and by this time she had her mind set on becoming a professional writer. It was also the first occasion on which she used the pseudonym "K. Mansfield".


E


Mansfield rapidly grew discontented with the provincial New Zealand lifestyle, and with her family.  Two years later she headed again in London. Her father sent her an annual subsidy of €100 for the rest of her life. In later years, she would express both admiration and disdain for New Zealand in her journals.


F

In 1911, Mansfield met John Middleton Murry, the Oxford scholar and editor of the literary magazine Rhythm. They were later to marry in 1918. Mansfield became a co-editor of Rhythm, which was subsequently called The Blue Review, in which more of her works were published. She and Murry lived in various houses in England and briefly in Paris. The Blue Review failed to gain enough readers and was no longer published. Their attempt to set up as writers in Paris was cut short by Murry's bankruptcy , which resulted from the failure of this and other journals. Life back in England meant frequently changed addresses and very limited funds.


G

Between 1915 and 1918, Mansfield moved between England and Bandoi, France. She and Murry developed close contact with other well-known writers of the time such as D.H. Lawrence , Bertrand Russell and Aldous Huxley. By October 1918 Mansfield had become seriously ill; she had been diagnosed with tuberculosis and was advised to enter a sanatorium. She could no longer spend time with writers in London. In the autumn of 1918, she was so ill that she decided to go to Ospedale in Italy. It was the publication of Bliss and Other Stories in 1920 that was to solidify Mansfield's reputation as a writer.


H


Mansfield also spent time in Menton, France, as the tenant of her father's cousin at "The Villa Isola Bella". There she wrote eight stories including Miss Britt and The Daughters of the Later Colonel, the latter of which she pronounced to be"... the only story that satisfies me to any extent".


I


Mansfield produced a great deal of work in the final years of her life, and much of her prose and poetry remained unpublished at her death in 1923. After her death, her husband, Murry, took on the task of editing and publishing her works.  His efforts resulted in two additional volumes of short stories. The Doves' Nest and Something Childish, published in 1923 and 1924 respectively, the publication of her Poems as well as a collection of critical writings (Novels and Novelist) and a number of editions Mansfield's previously unpublished letters and journals.

groups.title: Questions 1-13
groups.source_html:
Questions 1 - 6


Choose TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text, choose FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, or choose NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

1

The name Katherine Mansfield, that appears on the writer's book, was exactly the same as her origin name.

正确答案： B. FALSE

定位关键词：

Katherine Mansfield；writer's book；origin name


原文：

A段，“She became one of New Zealand's best-known writers, using the pen name of Katherine Mansfield.”



解析：

题干说Katherine Mansfield这个名字出现在作者的书里，和她的本名一模一样，但原文A段提到Katherine Mansfield这个名字是作为笔名，所以题干信息与原文信息不符，答案为FALSE。

2

Mansfield won a prize for a story she wrote for Maori people for the High School Reporter.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

定位关键词：

Mori；High School Reporter


原文：

B段，“Her first published stories appeared in the High School Reporter and the Wellington Girls' High School magazine in 1898 and 1899.”


解析：

题干说Mansfield 为High School Reporter 写的一篇关于毛利人的报道获得了奖项，但原文B段只提到在该杂志上发表文章，并未提到是否是关于毛利人的报道，也并未提到是否获得奖项，所以答案为NG。

3

How Pearl Button Was Kidnapped portrayed Maori people in a favorable way.

正确答案： A. TRUE

定位关键词：

How Pearl Button Was Kidnapped；Maori 


原文：

B段，“in general terms of her interest in the Maori people (New Zealand's native people), who were often portrayedin a sypathetic light in her later stories, such as How Pearl Button Was Kidnapped.”


解析：

题干说How Pearl Button Was Kidnapped以一种有利的方式描绘了毛利人，原文B段提到在这个故事中毛利人经常被描绘成同情的形象，意思就是会将他们以正面形象呈现给读者，两者表述相符，所以答案为TRUE。

4

When Mansfield was at Queen's College, she planned to be a professional writer.

正确答案： B. FALSE

定位关键词：

Queen's College


原文：

C段，“Mansfield recommenced playing the cello, an occupation that she believed, during her time at Queen's, she would take up professionally.”


解析：

题干说当Mansfield在女王学院读书时，她打算成为一名职业作家，原文C段提到Mansfield在女王学院学习期间会把大提琴作为自己的职业，而不是成为一个作家，所以本题答案是FALSE。

5

Mansfield was unpopular with the other students at Queen's College.

正确答案： B. FALSE

定位关键词：

Queen's College


原文：

C段，“she was appreciated amongst fellow students at Queen's for her lively and charismatic approach to life and work.”



解析：

题干说Mansfield在女王学院读书时是不受同学欢迎的，原文C段提到Mansfield在生活和工作中充满活力和魅力，受到女王大学同学们的赞赏，题干与原文不符，答案为FALSE。

6

In London, Mansfield showed little interest in politics.

正确答案： A. TRUE

定位关键词：

London；politics


原文：

C段，“Mansfield did not actively support the suffragette movement in the UK. Women in New Zealand had gained the right to vote in 1893.”



解析：

题干说Mansfield在伦敦时对政治不感兴趣，原文C段提到Mansfield并没有积极支持英国的妇女参政运动，所以题干与原文信息符合，答案为TRUE。

Questions 7 - 13


Complete the notes. Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER in each gap.

7

Katherine Mansfield's adult years

•

   - moved from England back to New Zealand

正确答案： 7 1906
Question 7

定位关键词：

England；New Zealand


原文：

D段，“After finishing her schooling in England, she returned to her New Zealand home in 1906”


解析：

此空根据整道题的结构形式，黑点处应该填年份，并且是Mansfield从英国回新西兰的年份。根据原文D段可知她在 1906年回新西兰，所以答案为1906。

8

   - first paid writing work was in a publication based in  

正确答案： 8 Australia
Question 8

定位关键词：

first paid writing work


原文：

D段，“She had several works published in Australia in a magazine called Native Comparison, which was her first paid writing work06”


解析：

此空要填的是第一份有偿工作是在哪里的出版社，根据原文D段可知她在澳大利亚的一本名为Native Comparison的杂志上发表了几篇作品，这是她第一次有偿写作，所以这家出版社在澳大利亚，此空答案为Australia。

9

   - her   and the New Zealand way of life made her feel dissatisfied

正确答案： 9 family
Question 9

定位关键词：

New Zealand way of life


原文：

E段，“Mansfield rapidly grew discontented with the provincial New Zealand lifestyle, and with her family.”


解析：

此空要填的是她的什么和新西兰的生活方式使她不舒服，根据原文E段可知，Mansfield对新西兰乡下人的生活方式和她的家人感到不满，所以此空应该填family。


同义替换：

discontented=dissatisfied

way of life=lifestyle

10

• 1908

   - returned to London

• 1911-1919

   - met John Middleton Murry in 1911

   - perverted Mansfield and Murry from staying together in Paris

正确答案： 10 bankruptcy
Question 10

定位关键词：

1911；Murry；Paris


原文：

F段，“Their attempt to set up as writers in Paris was cut short by Murry's bankruptcy”


解析：

此题需要填什么使Mansfield和Murry无法在巴黎一起生活。根据前面的1911定位到F段首句，空格处应该在此段后面，根据倒数第二句可得，他们在巴黎当作家的企图因Murry的破产而夭折，所以此空应该填bankruptcy。

11

   - spent time with distinguished  

   - from 1916, tuberculosis restricted the time she spent in London

正确答案： 11 writers
Question 11

定位关键词：

spent time


原文：

G段，“She and Murry developed close contact with other well-known writers of the time such as D.H. Lawrence,”


解析：

此题需要填一起度过时间的对象是什么。根据原文G段可知，Mansfield和Murry与当时的其他知名作家保持密切联系，此空应该填writers。


同义替换：

distinguished=well-known

12

• 1920

   - her   was consolidated when Bliss and Other Stories was published

   - wrote several stories at "Villa Isola Bella"

正确答案： 12 reputation
Question 12

定位关键词：

1920；Bliss and Other Stories


原文：

G段，“It was the publication of Bliss and Other Stories in 1920 that was to solidify Mansfield's reputation as a writer.”


解析：

此题需要填在Bliss and Other Stories出版之后她的什么得到了巩固。根据原文G段最后一句可知，1920年出版的Bliss and Other Stories巩固了Mansfield作为作家的声誉。所以此空填reputation。


同义替换：

solidify=consolidate

13

• 1923-1924

   - Mansfield's   published more of her works after her death

正确答案： 13 husband
Question 13

定位关键词：

published more of her works；death


原文：

I段，“After her death, her husband, Murry, took on the task of editing and publishing her works.”


解析：

此题需要填在Mansfield死后是谁给她出版了更多作品 。根据原文I段可知，此处填husband。
